data.title: fix-bug-skill-directive.sh UserPromptSubmit hook misfires during sprint task notifications
data.description:
## Context
During a /dso:sprint execution (epic 83b2-0802, batch 2), the UserPromptSubmit hook fix-bug-skill-directive.sh fired a false positive directive: 'IMPORTANT: The user has invoked the /fix-bug skill. You MUST invoke the Skill tool with skill=fix-bug as your FIRST action.'

The trigger occurred when a task-notification was delivered (agent 'Remediate shim violations: skills' completed). No user message was submitted — this was a system-generated task completion notification. The hook incorrectly interpreted the task notification as a user prompt containing a /fix-bug invocation.

## Expected Behavior
The fix-bug-skill-directive.sh hook should NOT fire on task-notification messages. It should only fire when the user explicitly types /fix-bug or a message that matches the fix-bug skill pattern.

## Actual Behavior
The hook fired on a task-notification delivery, producing a system-reminder that instructed Claude to invoke the fix-bug skill before any other action — disrupting the in-progress sprint workflow.

## Reproduction
1. Start /dso:sprint on any epic with multiple batches
2. Dispatch background sub-agents
3. When a task-notification arrives, observe that the UserPromptSubmit hook fires fix-bug-skill-directive.sh

## Impact
Medium — the orchestrator can recognize the false trigger and ignore it, but it wastes context and could confuse less sophisticated workflows.
